documentation_links.title: CloudWatch Synthetics Python Selenium runtime versions

recommendations.problem: The log level bug exists only in syn-python-selenium-7.0

solution.description: Update the canary to use syn-python-selenium-8.0 or later, which fixes the log level for 'Loading was canceled' messages
solution.expected_outcome: The 'Loading was canceled' message will be logged at WARNING level and will not cause canary failure

recommendations.title: Upgrade to syn-python-selenium-8.0 or later

recommendations.problem: If upgrading to 8.0 is not immediately possible

solution.description: Downgrade the canary back to syn-python-selenium-6.0 to restore the original WARNING log level behavior
solution.expected_outcome: The canary will pass again as 'Loading was canceled' is logged at WARNING level

recommendations.title: Downgrade to syn-python-selenium-6.0 as a temporary workaround

root_cause: syn-python-selenium-7.0 reclassified the Chromium browser message 'Loading was canceled' from WARNING to ERROR level. The Synthetics runtime treats any ERROR-level log entry as a canary failure indicator. Since 'Loading was canceled' is a benign Chromium event that occurs during normal page navigation (especially redirects), canaries that previously passed now fail without any actual endpoint or script issue. Fixed in syn-python-selenium-8.0, which restores the WARNING level.
symptoms: Canary starts failing after upgrading to syn-python-selenium-7.0 from an earlier Selenium runtime
Canary logs show 'Loading was canceled' at ERROR level instead of WARNING
Canary reports failure even though the page loads successfully
No code changes were made — only the runtime version was updated
title: syn-python-selenium-7.0 changes 'Loading was canceled' log level from WARNING to ERROR causing canary failures
